contradiction_text: The reported 65.4% benchmark result is presented as strong, but the source also says rollout outcomes are unstable enough that sampled runs can differ materially, which weakens confidence in fine-grained leaderboard precision.
related_claim_texts: Augment reports that its SWE-bench Verified submission achieved a 65.4% success rate using Claude Sonnet 3.7 as the core driver and OpenAI o1 as the ensembler.
The source indicates that SWE-bench results are materially bounded by benchmark instability and task-domain specificity, limiting clean transfer to broader real-world coding work.
disclosure: Keep visible that the headline score is self-reported and sits on top of benchmark instability, so small performance margins should not be treated as clean evidence of stable superiority.
